entries.title: Iran says US has responded to its latest peace proposal
entries.snippet: The US is yet to formally confirm it has responded. However, Trump reportedly told Israel's Kan News it was unacceptable.

entries.title: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
entries.snippet: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

entries.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
entries.snippet: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.

entries.title: Russian strikes kill 10 as Zelensky says Ukraine hits oil tankers and terminal
entries.snippet: Fatalities have been reported around Ukraine as Kyiv says Russian "shadow fleet" tankers were hit by drones.

entries.title: Nigeria summons South African envoy over attacks on its nationals
entries.snippet: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

entries.title: Police officer lowered into crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
entries.snippet: Police suspect the human remains belong to a businessman swept away by floodwater last week.

entries.title: The clandestine network smuggling Starlink tech into Iran to beat internet blackout
entries.snippet: Sahand tells the BBC World Service he sends satellite internet terminals into Iran to help show "the real picture".

entries.title: Germany troop cuts send wrong signal to Russia, say two top US Republicans
entries.snippet: The chairs of the House and Senate armed services committees said withdrawing 5,000 service personnel risked undermining deterrence.

entries.title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
entries.snippet: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.

entries.title: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
entries.snippet: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.

entries.title: Man charged with murder of 5-year-old Australian girl
entries.snippet: The girl's death sparked an outbreak of violence in the central Australian town from where she disappeared.

entries.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
entries.snippet: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

entries.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
entries.snippet: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

entries.title: Oil tanker hijacked off coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia
entries.snippet: It is the second time an oil tanker has been hijacked in the area in the past 10 days.

entries.title: US Supreme Court asked to restore abortion pill access
entries.snippet: A lower court has limited mail-order access to the abortion pill mifepristone, which the drug's maker says will cause "irreparable harm".

entries.title: Cuba condemns new US sanctions as 'illegal' and 'abusive'
entries.snippet: The new measures come on top of a US blockade of oil to Cuba that has caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages.

entries.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
entries.snippet: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

entries.title: Who shot a Secret Service officer at the Trump press dinner?
entries.snippet: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

entries.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
entries.snippet: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

entries.title: Ex-F1 driver and Paralympic champion Zanardi dies
entries.snippet: Former Formula 1 driver and Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies at the age of 59.

entries.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
entries.snippet: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

entries.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
entries.snippet: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

entries.title: Pellet found in Secret Service agent's vest links suspect to WHCD attack, Pirro says
entries.snippet: The pellet "definitively" links the suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, to the attack, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro told CNN on Sunday.

entries.title: 'I just want to see her again' says son of imprisoned Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi
entries.snippet: On Thursday, authorities in Myanmar claimed they had transferred Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest. Her son Kim Aris spoke to NPR about his doubts about the regime's account.

entries.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
entries.snippet: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.

entries.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
entries.snippet: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.

entries.title: How algorithms wreaked havoc with these workers' schedules and cut their pay
entries.snippet: Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.

entries.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
entries.snippet: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

entries.title: Bard College's president to retire after scrutiny of relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
entries.snippet: The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.

entries.title: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner
entries.snippet: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

entries.title: 1,500 beagles will find new homes after release from research facility
entries.snippet: The first beagles removed from a Wisconsin dog breeding and research facility that was the site of recent protests seemed to know right away that they were safe.

entries.title: Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak aboard cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
entries.snippet: A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organization and South Africa's Department of Health said Sunday.

entries.title: Former Malian minister and junta critic abducted from home by armed men, family tells AP
entries.snippet: A former Malian minister and critic of the ruling junta was abducted from his home by armed, hooded men, one of his family members told The Associated Press Sunday.

entries.title: Secret Service agent hit by buckshot from the gun of man charged in correspondents' dinner attack, prosecutor says
entries.snippet: Authorities have determined that buckshot from the gun of the man charged with trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in an attempt to kill President Donald Trump struck a Secret Service agent, according to the federal prosecutor overseeing the investi

entries.title: What Florida's extreme drought means for the upcoming hurricane season
entries.snippet: Florida is experiencing one of its worst droughts in decades, but Gov. Ron DeSantis says there could be a silver lining: a less active hurricane season. Does dry weather correlate with fewer hurricanes? PolitiFact says not typically.

entries.title: Amtrak may make it easier to bring guns on its trains
entries.snippet: Amtrak is considering allowing people to store guns in lockboxes on most of its trains, which critics say would weaken security measures that instead should be strengthened in light of the shooting at last weekend's White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

entries.title: Trump says U.S. will help 'guide' stranded ships from Strait of Hormuz starting Monday
entries.snippet: U.S. President Donald Trump announced a project that will start on Monday to help stranded ships leave the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has effectively closed, but offered few details.

entries.title: Austrian police arrest suspect in supermarket baby food poisoning case
entries.snippet: Police in eastern Austria say a 39-year-old suspect has been arrested after rat poison turned up in some HiPP baby food jars on supermarket shelves in central Europe.

entries.title: Two U.S. service members missing in Morocco following annual military exercises
entries.snippet: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said Sunday.

entries.title: Trump says he is reviewing new Iranian proposal to end war
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he was reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war.

entries.title: State redistricting battles intensify following U.S. Supreme Court ruling on Voting Rights Act
entries.snippet: A Supreme Court decision striking down a majority Black congressional district in Louisiana has amplified an already intense national redistricting battle. Here's a look at how some states are responding.

entries.title: After Spirit Airlines shutdown, how passengers can get home and get refunds
entries.snippet: If you've been snagged in Spirit Airlines' now-defunct flight schedule, here are some things to know on how to get home, and get whole.

entries.title: Expensive tickets for early World Cup games still on general sale
entries.snippet: Tickets for most of the World Cup group games remain on general sale with just over a month to go until the tournament kicks off on June 11. But prices are exorbitantly high.

entries.title: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalized after health crisis in prison
entries.snippet: Iran's imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to a hospital in northwestern Iran after a "catastrophic deterioration" of her health, her foundation said Friday.

entries.title: Diet soda's mixed science and how to kick the habit
entries.snippet: The research on diet sodas has about as much clarity as a glass of Diet Coke. Here's a quick guide by PolitiFact to navigating the low-calorie beverage menu — and weaning yourself off sweet, fizzy sodas, if you're ready.

entries.title: Mexican governor and mayor step down after U.S. drug trafficking indictments
entries.snippet: Two members of Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum's party in the northwestern Sinaloa state said they would temporarily step down from their posts after the United States charged them and eight other politicians and security officers with drug trafficking.

entries.title: Trump's new surgeon general nominee has both praised and criticized his administration
entries.snippet: Dr. Nicole Saphier is President Donald Trump's latest pick for the vacant role of U.S. surgeon general, a nomination that ended the embattled campaign of his previous candidate, Dr. Casey Means.

entries.title: Spirit Airlines goes out of business after 34 years, ceases operations immediately
entries.snippet: Spirit Airlines, an impish upstart that shook the industry with its irreverent ads and deep discount fares, announced Saturday that it has gone out of business after 34 years.

entries.title: What to know about a mifepristone maker asking the Supreme Court to restore access to the pill by mail
entries.snippet: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier. Here's what to know.

entries.title: U.S. to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany in next 6 to12 months
entries.snippet: The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany in the next six to 12 months, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Merz 'not giving up' on Germany's relationship with US
entries.snippet: The German chancellor said there was "no connection" between disagreements over the Iran war and the pullout of US troops from Germany. He insisted he shared Trump's goal of ensuring Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon.

entries.title: Nigeria summons South Africa envoy over xenophobic incidents
entries.snippet: Nigeria joins other international voices warning against rising anti-migrant sentiment targeting Africans in South Africa.

entries.title: How Serbia's government dominates the country's media
entries.snippet: Since Serbia's ruling SNS party came to power in 2012, it has tightened its grip on the media. With an election expected soon, experts fear authorities will try to eliminate the last pockets of independent reporting.

entries.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia's Primorsk oil terminal
entries.snippet: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv also hit two of Russia's shadow fleet tankers near the Black Sea port of Novorossiysk. Two sides have continued to fire hundreds of drones at each other on a daily basis.

entries.title: Philippines' Mayon volcano spews ash — Pictures
entries.snippet: The most active volcano in the Philippines is erupting, sending lava down its slope and covering surrounding villages in ash.

entries.title: Germany news: Majority thinks coalition government doomed — survey
entries.snippet: More than half of people in Germany believe the coalition government will collapse before its term is up. The head of a leading economic think tank says US tariffs could trigger a German recession.

entries.title: Millions sway as free Shakira concert lights up Copacabana
entries.snippet: The Colombian superstar delivers one of the biggest shows of her career, as fans flock from Brazil and beyond for her "Women No Longer Cry" world tour.

entries.title: Trump says cutting US troops in Germany 'a lot further'
entries.snippet: Germany's defense minister has said Europe needs to take more responsibility for its own security. Top Republicans warn reducing troop numbers sends the "wrong signal" to Russian President Vladimir Putin.

entries.title: Where Germany's economy still excels
entries.snippet: German industry is being lapped in key sectors like solar panels, semiconductors and even cars — but the country's midsize businesses suggest the economy isn't losing its edge.

entries.title: North Korea rejects US cybercrime claims as 'absurd slander'
entries.snippet: Pyongyang refutes allegations of hacking and crypto theft, even as a UN panel estimated billions stolen by North Korea-linked cyberattacks.

entries.title: Iran war: Trump says US to 'guide ships safely' out of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The US president did not elaborate on whether the plan was agreed upon with Iran, which has maintained de facto control over the strait. Tehran warned any interference in Hormuz would be a ceasefire violation. Follw DW.

entries.title: Why Russia's Northern Sea Route is a risk for global trade
entries.snippet: Russia wants its Northern Sea Route to become an important artery of global trade. However, the Arctic shortcut between Europe and Asia is fraught with both political and environmental hurdles.

entries.title: Austria: Police arrest suspect over poisoned HiPP baby food
entries.snippet: A 39-year-old man has been arrested in Austria for his alleged involvement in an attempt to blackmail a German baby food manufacturer.

entries.title: Mexican governor indicted by US steps down
entries.snippet: Sinaloa Governor Ruben Rocha Moya has been accused of protecting the powerful Sinaloa cartel and helping it smuggle drugs into the US, allegedly taking in millions of dollars in bribes.

entries.title: Why Ukraine and Israel are arguing over grain
entries.snippet: Cargo ships have docked in Haifa, Israel, carrying grain thought to originate from Russian-occupied parts of Ukraine. Kyiv considers the cargo to be stolen goods — but Israel says evidence is lacking.

entries.title: Taiwan's president visits Eswatini despite China's objection
entries.snippet: The visit of Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te to the African country was delayed after several countries reportedly withdrew permission for him to fly over their territories due to pressure from China.

entries.title: Mali probes soldiers over role in Jihadi attacks
entries.snippet: At least five soldiers and an exiled politician are being investigated over their alleged involvement in coordinated attacks across Mali last weekend.

entries.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
entries.snippet: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

entries.title: Iran: Jailed Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi hospitalized
entries.snippet: Iranian activist Narges Mohammadi is in hospital after suffering a "cardiac crisis" in prison, her family says. Mohammadi won the 2023 Nobel Peace Prize for her advocacy of women's rights and freedom.
